strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: A five-answer bank paired with three falsifying repairers is the strongest replicated mechanism so far at 33/72 pooled exact, and remains the stable comparison for new architectures.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Nine bases materially improved candidate-oracle coverage in Iteration 8, while selection failed to convert most of that coverage. Falsifying repair can both select an existing candidate and generate a corrected exact answer; withholding review when the base has a strict majority should reduce unnecessary reversals and calls without introducing domain-specific logic.
strategies.name: Majority-Gated Nine-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: A single answer-generating falsifier directly tests whether the pooled five-by-three repair mechanism retains useful correction power at six calls per activated case. Comparing it with the failed one-review selector isolates repair capability from reviewer replication.
strategies.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Committee fallback had a positive correction balance but compressed away most raw candidates and left constraint at zero. Allowing reviewers to repair rather than merely choose tests whether independent committee delegates provide useful diverse diagnoses while answer generation overcomes delegate-level candidate loss.
strategies.name: Committee-Triggered Falsifying Repair
